The Warehouse Automation Pain Point

Rigid Islands of Automation

Traditional warehouse systems waste vertical space, demand constant maintenance, and can't adapt without major construction. The industry is stuck.

Real Estate Crisis

Warehousing costs are rising. Traditional aisles and floor-based robots waste up to 60% of vertical volume — the most expensive space goes unused.

Operational Complexity

AGVs that only do one thing. Digging storage systems. They block movement, require charging downtime, and can't adapt to layout changes without major rework.

Operational Friction

Batteries die. Robots traffic-jam. "Digging" for items in deep bins slows fulfillment dramatically. Endless system integrations that never quite work together.

The Paradigm Shift

Logtopus is Physical AI for Space.

Just as humanoids are a physical AI platform for human tasks, Logtopus is a physical AI platform for the warehouse itself. We transform a passive storage space into a fully autonomous, dynamic robot.

The Brain (Software)

Autonomous spatial management that optimizes in real-time based on order flow — predicting demand before the picker even walks.

The Muscles (Hardware)

Grid-powered ceiling robots. No batteries, no charging, no swapping. Continuous 24/7 operation from grid power alone.

Validated by US Provisional Patent — Novel & Non-Obvious

Extreme Versatility

One system handles everything: standard Euro-boxes, heavy pallets, and odd-shaped anomalies — no SKU left behind.

Direct Access

"No-Digging necessary." Every item is accessible immediately. No reshuffling deep stacks to reach what you need.

Continuous Power

Powered directly by grid rails. 24/7 operation without charging breaks or battery management headaches.

Two-Phase Strategy

Strategic Roadmap

Phase 1

Performance
Short Term

Delivering the highest-density storage solution on the market. Validated mechanical architecture that solves the immediate "Real Estate vs. Speed" conflict for 3PLs and E-grocery.

Phase 2

The Platform
Long Term

The same ceiling grid hosts diverse robotic modules: Manufacturing, Assembly, Retail Display. Customers evolve operations via software updates — not construction crews.
